quiz.question:
Black defends d6. 

Consider king safety.

Evaluate piece activity as well as which side has more space. 

Who's better?

feedback.text:
White has more space. In fact, Black's light-squared bishop can't even come into the game. White is better. 

even e5 is an idea, exposing Black's setup.

White has more space, and Black's pieces can't really do much. 

Black is barely holding on because White controls all the pawn breaks.

Black has less space and less active pieces. Material is equal and king safety is equal.

It seems Black has a disadvantage according to two of the criteria.

This evaluation implies that you believe Black has more space and more active pieces. Both of those assumptions are false. 

The e4, d5 and a4 pawns give White more space. And White's pieces are more active.
